`, song: { title: "Impressive Instant", artist: "Madonna", cover: "https://resources.tidal.com/images/0a7e368b/665a/4d05/a8b4/23b6072193e9/320x320.jpg", url: ["bqw5Ki3XvUc", "oqqmRwcvsP4"] } }, { id: "obamna2", condition: (ev) => ev >= 535, image: "https://www.chicagotribune.com/wp-content/uploads/migration/2015/07/28/JAFLQF3XLJAVXFGHD4K7U6EKXM.jpg?w=1800&resize=1800,1800", html: `
37A.
54  55  EXT. HIGHWAY 15 — MOJAVE DESERT — EVENING54 55
The spy car streaks along.
BUGS/DAFFY  (O.S.)
(sings)
VIVA, LAS VEGAS/ VIVA —
INT. SPY CAR — CONTINUOUS54 55
Bugs FLAILS on a banjo as he sings. Stuckmann grabs the banjo and throws it out the window.
BUGS
You've got no music in your soul, you know that?
STUCKMANN
I'm uh, aware of that, yes.
Bugs shrugs and turns to the dashboard. It's a dizzying array of hardware. Bugs' hand hovers over the switches.
BUGS
Jeesh, he went for all the options. Chump.
STUCKMANN
Don't touch anything. This is Damian Drake's car, and if—
Bugs flicks a switch. Through the back windshield we see two white jet streams shoot out. A moment later, two huge fireballs erupt a half mile back.
Bugs scans the array of buttons.
STUCKMANN
If you, um, touch one more button —
BUGS
Shh. I'm about to defy you.
(CONTINUED)
38.
CONTINUED:
Bugs reaches. Stuckmann blocks him. In doing so, he places the palm of his hand flat on a black panel on the dashboard. The panel glows.*
Stuckmann quickly pulls his hand away, but a perfect fingerprint map remains. Across the bottom of the panel flashes the words "SEARCHING..."
BUGS
(CONT'D)
Now you done it.
The handprint disappears. It is replaced by Stuckmann's government ID photo surrounded by a dizzying scroll of personal information: height, weight, security clearance levels, cholesterol, library fines, etc.
FEMALE COMPUTER VOICE  (V.O.)
Focko Reginald Stuckmann
BUGS
I can deal with Focko, but Reginald?
STUCKMANN
My grandmother named me.
Assorted photos flash on screen: a one-year-old Stuckmann in a bath, a ten-year-old Stuckmann at a model UN conference, a sixteen-year-old Stuckmann at a debate tournament holding a third-place ribbon.
FEMALE COMPUTER VOICE  (V.O.)
IQ, one hundred and forty-seven. Spatial IQ, sixty-three.
STUCKMANN
(measured)
Heh. The, um, spatial test was administered following a red-eye flight.
FEMALE COMPUTER VOICE  (V.O.)
In high school, he was president of the student council. He prepared a forty-five minute victory speech, cut short by thirty-nine minutes.
STUCKMANN
There were, uh, important points that needed to be made.
BUGS
I bet.
On the screen is a full-length picture of Stuckmann. Various parts of his body and face light up in red. The title over the illustration reads "PROBLEM AREAS." Stuckmann's finger pushes the button next to the touch screen. Instead of stopping, A FLURRY OF ROBOTIC ARMS shoot out of the dash and attack Stuckmann and Bugs. Stuckmann screams.
56  57  EXT. HIGHWAY 1556 57
The car swerves all over the road.
INT. SPY CAR — MOVING55 56 57
The robot arms retract, revealing a dishevelled Stuckmann and Bugs, now wearing full scuba gear.
FEMALE COMPUTER VOICE  (V.O.)
Freshman year of college, spent eleven weekends attempting to dunk a basketball on a regulation hoop. He managed it once.
STUCKMANN
(quietly)
Hey! It was a personal achievement...
Annoyed, Stuckmann pushes the button again. The robotic arms flurry in again, leaving Stuckmann in a perfectly pressed evening suit and Bugs in a tuxedo.
FEMALE COMPUTER VOICE  (V.O.)
(CONT'D)
Estimated number of times he will run for president: sixty-seven.
57A  58  58A  EXT. HIGHWAY 1557A 58 58A
The car drives off as we hear...
STUCKMANN  (O.S.)
Let me be clear... that was an invasion of... privacy.
BUGS  (O.S.)
Man, I love this show.


`, song: { title: "Dream On Dreamer", artist: "The Brand New Heavies", cover: "https://yt3.googleusercontent.com/XEBsqlUnZQtyxH1ZKCDbjQ-xkyrAdpmPg-iosU4ilHNRIkGC6_VMxWtFaL4I9HG0WYCCAR9qeW7oqoiA=w544-h544-l90-rj", url: ["cWWKPgmNFvI", "KQsfCrrfDlU"] } }, { id: "dubya", condition: (ev) => ev >= 0 && ev < 4, image: "https://www.thesmokinggun.com/sites/default/files/imagecache/750x970/documents/shower.jpg", html: `
any ole love

The water was scalding hot, beating a relentless, drumming rhythm against his shoulders. Steam billowed in thick, humid clouds toward the exhaust fan of the White House master bathroom, turning the pale tiles into a slick, weeping canvas of condensation.

George stood under the spray, his head bowed, letting the heat seep into his neck. It was weird to think he was in his second term now. The maps from election night were still burned into the back of his eyelids every time he blinked. Oh boy, over five hundred and thirty-five electoral votes. Twice, even… it was a completely unified map. A sea of solid, unbroken red that stretched from the Pacific to the Atlantic, leaving absolutely zero room for doubt.

It was a mandate so total, so historically absolute, that it felt less like a political victory to him, and more like a terrifying, suffocating cage. He rubbed the water out of his eyes; the soap was stinging slightly. It was right then, beneath the roar of the showerhead, that he felt it.

A creeping, prickling sensation at the base of his skull. Someone was here. He was being watched.

◇ ◇ ◇

He stiffened. How silly this was…! The Secret Service was stationed outside the doors, of course. Nobody could be in here. But the weight of the gaze was palpable, boring into the wet skin of his back. It felt like the entire country was crowded into the steam-filled room with him, waiting for him to do something profound.

Slowly, George turned his head.

He looked toward the small, circular shaving mirror mounted on the tiled wall. The glass was heavily fogged, but the steam had begun to bead and roll down the surface, clearing a jagged little window in the center.

There was a face in the glass, looking back at him over his own shoulder. Hmm.

He stared at it. The face stared back. It was framed by wet, graying hair, the eyes slightly bloodshot from the shampoo, and the expression tired and remarkably ordinary. This guy, well, he didn’t look like an emperor. He didn’t look like a man who had conquered the electoral map in a feat unseen since George Washington either. He looked small.

A strange, quiet thought bubbled up through the white noise of the falling water. Oh, if only he had been less milquetoast.

If only he had possessed the booming gravity of LBJ, or the cinematic charm of Reagan. If only he felt like he actually inhabited the monumental space he now occupied! Instead, he had coasted into history on a wave of zingers, folksy half-sentences, and a country too exhausted to disagree. He had won the whole world, surely, and he still just felt like he’d wandered into the wrong high-stakes poker game and accidentally taken the pot.

If only he was more like his father…

George raised his right hand. He extended his index finger, slowly bridging the gap between himself and the glass. He felt the sudden, irrational fear that his hand might just pass right through, or that the reflection would do something else entirely. Oh God, what if it happened again? Just like last time…?

His finger touched the mirror.

The cold, hard surface of the glass met the warm, wet pad of his skin. Through the clearing fog, the reflection’s finger matched his own, perfectly, point for point. Surface meeting surface, reality meeting reality.

It was him. It was just him.

George stood there for a few seconds, listening to the water hit the tiles. He looked at the perfect, unbroken symmetry of the touch. Slowly, he pulled his hand away. He let his arm drop back to his side, turning his face back into the spray of the water.

“Huh,” he said softly to the empty room.

“Who are we?”

`, song: { title: "My Father's Son", artist: "Conner Reeves", cover: "https://yt3.googleusercontent.com/xpXX3fO5Mk_8ERanLYNQrAhyftJfimb9FxNfju03o2vAlStE1oGuNp5ePs42HgwLOw4D1oGwkaI7WDjb=w544-h544-l90-rj", url: ["_vM6gdOoOeY"] } }, // surprise surprise { id: "charlieextra", condition: (ev) => charlieWin && linus_bond > 0, image: "https://m.media-amazon.com/images/M/MV5BYTYyZjIzMmQtNmVmNy00ZDliLWI4ZWUtNzU4MjBhNjM1N2Q1XkEyXkFqcGc@._V1_.jpg", html: `

11:49p sgtPatty: marcie you cannot call me sir on the internet we talked about this

11:49p salprincess_xoxo: HI HI HI!! i had to get off the phone sorry i'm late. who's all here?? oh hi schroeder <3 not that i was looking for anyone in particular of course...

romanceofRome has entered the room.

` }, { id: "penpal", condition: (ev) => linus_bond <= 0, image: "https://i.pinimg.com/1200x/5f/b8/e2/5fb8e2357128f5bab5ec5fe836e36fed.jpg", html: `
☾
boy strange

The envelope had been sitting on his desk for three hours.

Charlie Brown had found it jammed at the bottom of the mailbox that afternoon, wedged beneath a grocery circular. The return address was blank. The handwriting on the front, however, was unmistakable. He hadn’t seen that precise, slightly slanted script in years.

He sat on the edge of his bed, the room washed in the cold, pale blue of moonlight filtering through the windowpanes. He was trembling. His last letter to his pen-pal had been a frantic, messy confession. He had written it at two in the morning, shaking and crying, outlining every ounce of his ruined friendship with Linus and the suffocating isolation of the school hallways, and confessing the terrifying, absolute certainty that the world would be lighter without him in it. And it was a message sent into the void, nothing more than that; he did not expect a reply.

He swallowed hard, carefully sliding his thumb under the flap of the envelope. Charlie pulled out two sheets of blue stationery, unfolded them, and began to read.

“Dear Charlie Brown,”

“I was surprised to find an envelope with your name on it in my box after all this time. I thought you had forgotten about me. But my heart sank to the floor when I read the words inside.”

“You told me that you did something unforgivable. You said that you looked at your best friend, the boy who always carried a blanket, and you tore him down just so you could finally stand on a pedestal. That whatever victories you had in the last couple of months felt like poison in your veins. And that you are so exhausted by your own failures that you want to stop existing entirely.”

“I am writing this back to you immediately because I need you to listen to me: do not do it.”

Charlie squeezed his eyes shut. His chest hitched; it was a sharp, ragged sound breaking the silence of the bedroom.

“People make terrible, ugly mistakes when they are desperate to be seen,” the letter continued. “You ran a cruel campaign because you have spent your entire life being the punchline, and you finally wanted to be the hero. But true monsters do not lie awake in the dark weeping over the pain they have caused. And true monsters do not feel the agonizing regret that is radiating off the pages of your letter.”

“The fact that your soul is aching right now is the absolute proof that you are still a good person.”

· · ·

“You feel like you have ruined everything. Perhaps you have ruined this one thing. But life is vast, Charlie Brown, and your town is such a remarkably small piece of it. Do not let one terrible chapter convince you to burn the whole book.”

“Tomorrow will come. You will have to walk down those hallways, and you will have to face him, and worse, you will have to face yourself. It will be agonizing. You will have to live with the consequences of your ambition. But you will survive it. I know your friend will too. You just need time.”

“There is a deep, quiet grace in living with our mistakes and choosing to be better anyway. You owe it to Linus, and you owe it to yourself, to stay here and try to be better. You cannot fix the bridge if you are gone.”

“I don’t know if we will ever meet. But I have known you through your words for a very long time. I know the boy who manages the baseball team that never wins. I know the boy who loves his cute, erratic little dog. And I know the boy who never, ever gives up.”

“I trust that boy. I trust him to stay strong, I trust him to keep breathing. I feel for him, and I hope you will too.”

“Forgive yourself enough to stay alive, Charlie Brown. Please.”

“Always, your pen-pal.”

The pages slipped from Charlie’s fingers, drifting like blue leaves to the floorboards.

He didn’t try to catch them this time. He just sat there, with his hands empty, and letting the words echo in the hollowed-out spaces of his chest. For a moment, the room was perfectly still. And then, Charlie slid off the edge of the mattress, falling to his knees on the cold wood, and wept.

He cried for the things he couldn’t take back, for his dog, for his friend, for his friendships, and he cried for the terrible weight he had been carrying, but as he gasped for air in the dark, he realized something profound: he was still breathing.

And as he cried there in the dark, the moonlight caught the edge of the fallen paper. He didn’t feel entirely fixed, and he knew tomorrow would be incredibly hard. But for the first time in a very long time, as he sat there in the quiet, Charlie Brown wanted to see the morning.

` }, // even more extras oh my god { id: "transcript_patty", condition: (ev) => charlieWin && patty_approval >= 2, image: "https://i.pinimg.com/1200x/90/f6/ab/90f6aba7514b426faf4da80231a05b2d.jpg", html: `
☎
precious box
DATE: NOVEMBER 14, 2001
TIME: 18:42 EST
ORIGIN: PAYPHONE, GREENWICH VILLAGE, NYC
DESTINATION: DORMITORY LANDLINE, PROVIDENCE, RI
MARCIE:
Hello?
PATTY:
Hey, uh, it’s me. I only have like two dollars in quarters, so talk fast.
MARCIE:
Good evening, Sir. How is New York treating you?
PATTY:
Pretty loud, pretty expensive. But I found that record store you told me about, the one down in the Village. It smells like dust in here, so I think you’d absolutely love it here.
MARCIE:
I am perfectly capable of appreciating dust, Sir. Did you find what you were looking for?
PATTY:
Yeah. Found an original pressing of that old-school R&B record I wanted, the D’Angelo one.
[sound of traffic passing; a muffled siren in the background]
I don’t know, Marce. Holding it just made me think of last year, how insane we all were.
MARCIE:
I believe ‘clinically dramatic’ covers it, yes.
PATTY:
Right? Like, I ran into Schroeder last week. He’s at Juilliard now, and he’s walking around acting like he personally invented the piano. And Lucy is already threatening her pre-law professors. It’s like nobody learned anything.
MARCIE:
Some people are remarkably resistant to personal growth. I assume our friend Linus is currently writing a four-hundred-page thesis at Yale on how losing a high school election built his moral character?
PATTY:
[Patty laughs; it’s a bright, genuine sound cutting through the payphone static]
Oh, totally. He’s probably quoting Kierkegaard to freshmen who just want to do keg stands or whatever.
MARCIE:
Has Charles called you recently?
PATTY:
Yeah, last Tuesday. He sounds… okay. Still overthinking everything, honestly. He won the whole damn school and he still thinks he broke the world doing it. The poor guy, he just can’t let himself be happy. Calgary doesn’t help either.
MARCIE:
[a soft sigh over the line]
We were all taking ourselves so seriously. It was just student council!
PATTY:
It felt like the whole world at the time, though. Didn’t it?
MARCIE:
[a long pause]
Yes. It did.
PATTY:
Yeah.
[another pause; a subway train rumbles faintly in the distance]
It’s really loud here, Marcie. And it’s freezing.
MARCIE:
New York usually is. You should go back to your dorm before you catch a cold.
PATTY:
I kinda wish you were here to complain about it with me.
MARCIE:
[her voice softens considerably]
I wish I was there too, Patty.
PATTY:
[automated operator voice interrupts: “Please deposit twenty-five cents for the next three minutes.”]
Shit, I’m out of time.
MARCIE:
Buy the record, go get warm. Call me on Sunday from the dorm landline?
PATTY:
It’s a date. See you, Marce.
[CONNECTION TERMINATED]
` }, { id: "transcript_lucy", condition: (ev) => charlieWin && support_arts >= 2, image: "https://i.pinimg.com/1200x/1f/ae/9b/1fae9b86776deb5a65383c3a0a7f42fd.jpg", html: `
☎
fine time
DATE: DECEMBER 03, 2001
TIME: 21:14 EST
ORIGIN: PRACTICE ROOM 4B, THE JUILLIARD SCHOOL, NYC
DESTINATION: OFF-CAMPUS APARTMENT, BOSTON, MA
LUCY:
Hello? Who is this?
SCHROEDER:
[the faint, solitary sound of a piano chord being struck]
You know me.
LUCY:
Schroeder? What, are you calling me voluntarily? Did your piano catch fire? Did someone finally tell you that wearing black turtlenecks every day makes you look like a mime?
SCHROEDER:
Very funny. They are visually unobtrusive and help me focus on the music, you know that. I was just organizing some sheet music and realized I hadn’t spoken to your brother recently. I assume Linus is surviving Yale?
LUCY:
Yeah, he’s fine. He joined a debate society just so he has an excuse to argue with people who are legally obligated to listen to him. Like, oh wow, he has all his rich hippie friends to hang out with now. Why didn’t you just call him yourself?
SCHROEDER:
He talks too much. I only have fifteen minutes before I need to review my counterpoint seminar notes.
LUCY:
Uh-huh.
SCHROEDER:
[another chord is struck, slightly dissonant]
Anyway… how is everyone else? Did the high school burn down after we left, or did Charlie actually manage to keep the lights on through graduation?
LUCY:
[a sharp, knowing exhale over the line]
Ah. There it is.
SCHROEDER:
There what is?
LUCY:
What’s this question even for? You haven’t asked about my brother in six months, Schroeder. And now suddenly you’re spending your precious practice time doing a wellness check on the former student council. You want to know about poor lttle Charlie.
SCHROEDER:
I am merely making polite conversation about our former classmates. It’s a sociological curiosity, really. Besides, I do find the trajectory of mediocre student governance intellectually stimulating.
LUCY:
You are a terrible liar. It’s a miracle you can play the piano with fingers that clumsy.
SCHROEDER:
[silence for a long moment, save for the ambient hum of city traffic outside his window]
…is he alright?
LUCY:
He’s fine. He’s at state college. He’s still a blockhead, obviously, but he’s getting by. He actually asked about you last week when I saw him at Thanksgiving.
SCHROEDER:
[the piano keys stop entirely]
Did he?
LUCY:
Yup. He asked if you were still hiding out in practice rooms pretending you don’t need other people. I told him yes. He seemed… disappointed. But not surprised, of course. Even he knows how you are.
SCHROEDER:
Ugh, I don’t hide. Music requires isolation, Lucy. It demands sacrifice of the ordinary—
LUCY:
Whatever you have to tell yourself to sleep at night, Bee-atch-thoven.
SCHROEDER:
[a heavy sigh]
You don’t have to go out of your way. But… if you happen to speak to him again, tell him I said hello. Okay? And tell him that he shouldn’t let the state college administration walk all over him. He earned his spine last year, he shouldn’t lose it now.
LUCY:
[her voice softens, losing its usual combative edge]
I’ll pass along the message. And try to get some fresh air, Schroeder. It’s bad for your complexion to live in the dark.
SCHROEDER:
Thank you, Lucy. I’ll try. Goodbye.
[CONNECTION TERMINATED]
` }, { id: "transcript_franklin", condition: (ev) => charlieWin && anti_establishment <= 2, image: "https://i.pinimg.com/1200x/74/26/9b/74269bc562c3a2927efacb9658f59211.jpg", html: `
✆
tape loop
DATE: FEBRUARY 12, 2002
TIME: 19:30 EST
ORIGIN: DORMITORY LANDLINE, GEORGETOWN UNIVERSITY, WASH. D.C.
DESTINATION: OFF-CAMPUS APARTMENT, STATE COLLEGE, PA
CHARLIE:
Hello?
FRANKLIN:
Charlie, it’s Frank. Uh, let me guess… you’re currently staring at a textbook trying to figure out how you got assigned thirty pages of reading for a Tuesday.
CHARLIE:
[the sound of a heavy textbook snapping shut]
Close. I was actually staring at the ceiling, smartass. Let me guess what you’re doing… you’re wearing a tie right now, aren’t you?
FRANKLIN:
[a low, tired chuckle]
It’s Georgetown, Charlie. If you wear sweatpants to the dining hall, they report you to the CIA. It’s a crazy nightmare, too. Like, I’m surrounded by five hundred kids desperately trying to be the next Linus.
CHARLIE:
Oh God. Well, tell me they don’t have the blankets.
FRANKLIN:
No blankets! But definitely the sweater vests. Everybody here wants to be a philosopher king.
CHARLIE:
I don’t miss that at all.
FRANKLIN:
You shouldn’t. Speaking of which, you know, I saw the retrospective Marcie published in her campus zine up at Brown. She dedicated a whole column to our “centrist coalition” actually!
CHARLIE:
[A pained groan]
Oh no. Did she call me a sellout?
FRANKLIN:
She called you a “pragmatist.”
CHARLIE:
That somehow hurts worse. Patty called me last week and she’s still mad we didn’t dismantle the entire administration. She said we had the mandate to burn Principal Othmar’s desk on the football field.
FRANKLIN:
Well, Patty is a wartime consigliere, Charlie. She doesn’t understand peacetime, you know she wanted a revolution. But you gave them functional heating in the library and a balanced student budget, so it’s not like it wasn’t worth it.
CHARLIE:
It just felt like giving up, sometimes. Just watering down the whole message just so the PTA wouldn’t panic…
FRANKLIN:
Listen to me. Revolutions look great on posters and all, but pragmatists actually keep people from freezing. The system is completely broken, sure, but you can’t fix the pipes if you refuse to walk into the building. You compromised, we worked together, and we got things done, that’s what matters. It’s not giving up, but it’s about, erm, growing up.
CHARLIE:
And where did you learn all this from?
FRANKLIN:
Kissinger.
CHARLIE:
[a long pause. the static hums quietly on the line]
Ah, thanks, Frank. I think… I really needed to hear that.
FRANKLIN:
Anytime, Mr. President. By the way, have you heard from Pig-Pen recently?
CHARLIE:
Yeah, actually. It’s ridiculous, but he’s following Radiohead on tour across the Midwest. The life of a roadie…
FRANKLIN:
Heh, some things never change. Get back to your reading, Charlie. I’ve got a macroeconomics paper to finish.
CHARLIE:
Good luck. And don’t let those kids get to you.
[CONNECTION TERMINATED]
` }, { id: "transcript_roy", condition: (ev) => charlieWin && window.isRoyInCharge, image: "https://i.pinimg.com/1200x/1f/f6/4f/1ff64f1ff28f354dddd8de6c316d81c4.jpg", html: `
☎
fate
DATE: APRIL 20, 2002
TIME: 02:14 MST
ORIGIN: CONVENIENCE STORE PAYPHONE, BOULDER, CO
DESTINATION: OFF-CAMPUS APARTMENT, STATE COLLEGE, PA
CHARLIE:
[a groggy, muffled rustling of sheets]
…hello?
ROY:
Chuck. Chuckster. My captain. El Presidente!
CHARLIE:
Roy? Do you have any idea what time it is? Are you high?
ROY:
What? N-no. No way, dude. I’m completely sober, have always been. My mind is a steel trap. I’m just… uh, you know, xperiencing the universe at a higher frequency right now.
CHARLIE:
[a heavy, tired sigh]
You called me at two in the morning to experience the universe at me?
ROY:
Hey, I’m at a payphone outside a 7-Eleven. There’s this funny neon sign here, and it’s buzzing, Chuck. It’s beautiful, sounds like a chorus of electric angels. But listen, that’s not why I called.
CHARLIE:
Then why did you call?
ROY:
I was just thinking about the campaign, man. Y’know, back in high school. It was all so cool, we really took down the establishment. We were like… Batman and Robin, but if Robin was super chill and had a much better CD collection. And was much cooler, too.
CHARLIE:
We ran for suburban high school student council, Roy.
ROY:
We changed history! And I just wanted to say… you’re a good guy, Chuck. You’re completely correctomundo. I miss you, man, I really do. I loved hanging out with you. I love you and stuff. You’re my brother.
CHARLIE:
[the line is quiet for a few seconds. When Charlie speaks again, the irritation is entirely gone from his voice]
I-I miss you too, Roy.
ROY:
Really? Because I feel like I messed things up a lot back then. Like with the registration forms, the flyters, uh… my general existence. Sometimes I think Patty was right and I was just dead weight.
CHARLIE:
Look, you weren’t dead weight. You kept me from losing my mind. We won, didn’t we?
ROY:
Yeah. We did.
CHARLIE:
Listen. Instead of calling me from a gas station in the middle of the night, why don’t you just pay me a visit? You can take the Greyhound down this weekend. Crash on my couch.
ROY:
[the sound of a plastic slurpee cup dropping onto the pavement]
Whoa, really? Are you serious?
CHARLIE:
Yeah. Just… don’t bring whatever you’re currently smoking.
ROY:
Dude, I told you, I’m just high on the crisp night air. And maybe a very… uh, large Blue Raspberry Slurpee. I’ll see you Saturday, my captain.
CHARLIE:
Goodnight, Roy. And drink some water, for God’s sake.
[CONNECTION TERMINATED]
` }, { id: "transcript_rerun", condition: (ev) => linusWin && linus_bond <= 2, image: "https://i.pinimg.com/1200x/0f/30/9d/0f309d9d86815f638d9e807247d3be26.jpg", html: `
☎
if
DATE: MAY 04, 2002
TIME: 16:12 EST
ORIGIN: VAN PELT RESIDENCE, IN SUBURBIA, MN
DESTINATION: DORMITORY LANDLINE, YALE UNIVERSITY, NEW HAVEN, CT
LINUS:
[the sound of pages being turned rapidly]
Hello?
RERUN:
Hey.
LINUS:
Rerun? Is everything alright? It’s the middle of the afternoon.
RERUN:
I just got home from school, Mom’s not here. I’m standing in your old room.
LINUS:
Have you been going through my things again? I cataloged all my belongings before I left for New Haven.
RERUN:
You don’t have any things. You took all your boring books and left the closet completely empty. It’s depressing.
LINUS:
It is called minimalism, Rerun. You’ll learn that when you get older. I find that an excess of material possessions tethers the mind to the—
RERUN:
[a loud, thoroughly unimpressed sigh]
Yeah, yeah, the earthly plane, whatever. You know what your problem is, Linus?
LINUS:
Problem? I was not aware I had a problem. I am currently maintaining a 3.9 GPA.
RERUN:
Oh you do. You won that stupid election two years ago, you got into the fancy college, you have everything you said you wanted… and you’re still acting like a sad little kid.
LINUS:
[the sound of pages turning abruptly stops]
Oh, please. That is a remarkably reductive assessment of my—
RERUN:
You’re hiding in another state because you’re scared of running into Charlie Brown at the grocery store. Come on. And now you’re using all your big words, thinking you’re some sort of sexy mofo, only so you don’t have to admit you’re just sitting in the dark feeling sorry for yourself.
LINUS:
I am not hiding. I am pursuing an elite secondary education. And I do not feel sorry for myself; unlike some of these losers, I am merely contemplating the inherent emptiness of institutional achievement.
RERUN:
You’re hiding. And you’re still holding onto that blanket, aren’t you?
LINUS:
I—
[a beat of profound, defensive silence]
Who told you that? It’s safely secured in a drawer.
RERUN:
You’re an adult now, Linus. Stop acting like you’re still eight, it’s exhausting for the rest of us.
LINUS:
…is there a reason you called to berate me?
RERUN:
Mom says you’re thinking about staying in Connecticut for the summer to do research. Please don’t.
LINUS:
I have opportunities here, brother. Grants! I am working closely with the philosophy department on a paper regarding moral absolutism.
RERUN:
Come home. You need to touch some actual grass and be normal for a minute. I’ll even let you teach me how to shoot a basketball.
LINUS:
You hate basketball.
RERUN:
I know, but you like it. So come home.
LINUS:
[the static hums quietly on the line, followed by a faint, reluctant exhale]
…I will keep that in mind.
RERUN:
Good. See ya.
LINUS:
Goodbye, Rerun.
[CONNECTION TERMINATED]
` }, { id: "transcript_maynard", condition: (ev) => linusWin && cynicism >= 4, image: "https://i.pinimg.com/1200x/f4/a2/ee/f4a2eed1056e98cff2416ccd83ec2a8b.jpg", html: `
☎
new agenda
DATE: OCTOBER 24, 2002
TIME: 14:12 EST
ORIGIN: PAYPHONE, NEW YORK STATE CAPITOL, ALBANY, NY
DESTINATION: LEGISLATIVE INTERN DESK, WASHINGTON, D.C.
MAYNARD:
[the sound of a heavy binder dropping onto a desk]
Senator Clinton’s office, how the fuck may I direct your call?
THIBAULT:
Jeez, Maynard. Drop the act, pick up the actual line. I just got thrown out of a state senator’s office. Literally, by a guy with a badge and everything.
MAYNARD:
Christ, Thibault. We talked about this. You can’t just threaten elected officials over municipal zoning reform and stuff. It’s bad optics, you gotta play the game. It’s as shrimple as that.
THIBAULT:
Are you seriously doing the fish puns again?
MAYNARD:
I’m running on three hours of sleep and bad coffee, let me have this. What did you even do?
THIBAULT:
I didn’t threaten him! I just told him his voting record on public infrastructure was dogshit and that he was a coward. Then I chucked my clipboard at his desk.
MAYNARD:
[a dry, exhausted sigh]
And you wonder why our grassroots initiatives are stalling. You lack finesse, man.
THIBAULT:
Oh, yeah? How’s your congressional gun control lobbying going, genius? Did the subcommittee read your massive fucking brief on closing the gun show loophole?
MAYNARD:
[the line goes stiffly silent for three seconds]
Look, they sent me a form letter. It thanked me for my ‘passion,’ it was signed by an autopen, and it included a complimentary bookmark featuring a bald eagle. They’ve gotta be squidding me.
THIBAULT:
Ah, screw you.
MAYNARD:
Hey, I spent eighty hours on that brief. I’m allowed to be a little crabby.
THIBAULT:
It’s all a joke, man. We’re nineteen, nobody gives a shit what we think.
[a loud echo of footsteps passing by the payphone]
Remember high school? Charlie Brown acting like a completely cynical psycho, running a Marxist revolution just to spite everybody? A-and Linus giving speeches like he’s fucking Cicero? We acted like we ran the shadow government.
MAYNARD:
Yeah, and now we’re just getting patted on the head by receptionists who call us ‘sweetie’, how wholesome. It’s degrading. Some people like that, actually.
THIBAULT:
I bet Linus isn’t getting thrown out of offices. He’s probably having sherry with the Dean of Yale right now, talking about ethics.
MAYNARD:
Actually, I read in the alumni newsletter that Linus is consulting for some think tank in New Haven. He’s totally fine. And Charlie is probably hiding under a rock in Pennsylvania, too depressed to even vote.
THIBAULT:
I’m going back to my dorm to play Halo. We’re wasting our time, I’m done.
MAYNARD:
Yeah, whatever. I intend to mail the committee another copy of my brief tomorrow morning anyway. With an expanded index. And let minnow if you get arrested, AG Spitzer can’t save your sorry ass every time.
THIBAULT:
Fuck off, Maynard.
[CONNECTION TERMINATED]
` }, { id: "transcript_shermy", condition: (ev) => charlieWin && ruthlessness >= 3, image: "https://i.pinimg.com/1200x/a7/f4/75/a7f4753ba12c224e153d6103c111b9de.jpg", html: `
☎
drive my car
DATE: OCTOBER 11, 2002
TIME: 02:40 EST
ORIGIN: WRITERS’ ANNEX, NBC STUDIOS, 30 ROCKEFELLER PLAZA, NYC
DESTINATION: BASEMENT APARTMENT, ASTORIA, QUEENS
SHERMY:
[a loud clatter as the receiver is fumbled, followed by a muffled groan]
I am asleep. Legally speaking, I am unconscious. If this isn’t an emergency involving free food, hang up.
CORMAC:
Wake up, man. I need a punchline. My monologue is dying, Conan is gonna fire me, and I’ll have to go back to selling my plasma to afford groceries.
SHERMY:
Ah, just write a joke about a guy walking into a bar with a duck. Or something about Jay Leno’s chin, audiences eat that shit up like it’s warm bread. It’s not hard.
CORMAC:
Too hack. Listen, I pitched a sketch based on our high school student council election today. I thought it was gold.
SHERMY:
[a heavy sigh, followed by the squeak of mattress springs]
D’oh. Why would you do that to yourself?
CORMAC:
I pitched a character based on Linus! Think of it, the weird intellectual kid in the sweater vest who carries a security blanket and talks like a 19th-century vicar. But the head writer told me it was “unrealistic” and “too cartoonish”. Bitch.
SHERMY:
Did you tell them about his sister charging five cents for psychiatric evaluations out of a cardboard box?
CORMAC:
I didn’t even get to the box! I just said “security blanket” and they shut me down. A bummer.
SHERMY:
What about Charlie Brown? Did you pitch him? You know, the bald kid who ran a cynical, mudslinging political machine that would make the mafia blush? I’d like that.
CORMAC:
I did! I said he literally ran a mob-style operation and the whole school bought it, hook, line, and sinker.
SHERMY:
They really did buy it hook, line, and sinker. It was terrifying.
CORMAC:
But the network standards guy said audiences wouldn’t buy a depressed teenager blackmailing his teachers to win an election. They said it was “too surreal” for the America of today, that S&P wouldn’t allow them. So now I’m stuck writing a bit about a talking pie.
SHERMY:
[a short, dark laugh]
We live in a Lynchian nightmare, Cormac. Our entire adolescence was a hallucination, normal adults simply cannot process it. Unlike them, we have seen the face of God, and it was a beagle flying a doghouse.
CORMAC:
Nobody believes me. I’m losing my mind up here at 30 Rock, I haven’t slept in three days.
SHERMY:
You need to try Ambien, man. Seriously, just take a pill and pass out. You’ll feel better.
CORMAC:
I tried Ambien. And then I hallucinated a giant yellow bird making fun of my script formatting, so never again.
SHERMY:
Right, well. Can I go back to sleep now? My mattress is like sixty percent springs and forty percent raccoon hair. I really need my rest if I’m gonna bomb at the Comedy Cellar tomorrow night.
CORMAC:
Yeah, yeah. Sleep well, you beautiful loser.
SHERMY:
Don’t let Conan eat you.
[CONNECTION TERMINATED]
` }, { id: "transcript_sally", condition: (ev) => linusWin && patty_approval < 3, image: "https://i.pinimg.com/1200x/78/89/05/7889058f3dca0508d970ca01d6e26156.jpg", html: `
✆
sending my love
DATE: MARCH 08, 2002
TIME: 15:30 TO 16:45 EST
ORIGIN: BROWN RESIDENCE LANDLINE
DESTINATION: VARIOUS RESIDENTIAL NUMBERS
SYSTEM:
[automated beep]
Call initiated to: EUDORA.
SALLY:
Eudora! Pick up the phone. It’s me, Sally. I am literally dying of boredom. My brother is staring at a wall again and my Sweet Babboo won’t call me back from college because he’s too busy being ‘intellectual.’ Call me back!
SYSTEM:
[automated beep]
Call initiated to: HAROLD ANGEL.
SALLY:
Harold! It’s Sally. Look, I know we haven’t talked since we were like, six, but I need someone to complain to. Answer your phone, please?
SYSTEM:
[automated beep]
Call initiated to: JOSÉ PETERSON.
SALLY:
José, seriously? You’re screening my calls now? I just want to talk about how annoying my life is. It’s not a big request, really. Just be a good friend and call me back.
SYSTEM:
[automated beep]
Call initiated to: LARRY.
SALLY:
[Voice rising in pitch]
Larry! If you are sitting there listening to my voice right now and choosing not to pick up, I swear to God I will come over there and break your answering machine. Don’t annoy me, for Christ’s sake.
SYSTEM:
[Automated beep]
Call initiated to: ETHAN.
SALLY:
Ethan. I know you’re there, nobody is this busy on a Tuesday afternoon. Pick. Up. The. Fucking. Phone.
SYSTEM:
[automated beep]
Call initiated to: “BADCALL” BENNY.
SALLY:
Benny, I am losing my mind! Am I invisible? Did I cease to exist? Do you even care about me? I am a very vibrant, interesting person and nobody is paying attention to me!
SYSTEM:
[automated beep]
Call initiated to: NAOMI.
SALLY:
[practically yelling]
Naomi! Screw you! You are officially off my Christmas list! You are all off my Christmas list!
SYSTEM:
[call connects; phone rings twice before being picked up.]
Call initiated to: JOE AGATE.
SALLY:
Joe, you absolute moron! Why is nobody answering my calls?! Do you all think you’re too good for me now? You play marbles in the dirt! You are the bottom of the social barrel! Talk to me! Wait until everyone knows you’re adopted and your “precious” little daddy is in the goddamn Cleveland Browns.
VIOLET:
[the sound of a magazine page turning lazily]
…excuse me?
SALLY:
[a sharp gasp of realization]
Wait. Who is this?
VIOLET:
This is Violet, Violet Gray. Who is this, and why are you screaming at me about marbles?
SALLY:
Violet?! Oh my god, I misdialed. I was trying to call Joe Agate.
VIOLET:
Uh, Sally? What, you were willingly trying to call Joe Agate? Jesus. Sally, your standards have truly plummeted since Linus left for college.
SALLY:
[flustered]
Shut up! I was just… just… checking my contacts! Sorry. Wrong number!
SYSTEM:
[loud click as Sally hangs up]
Party disconnected.
VIOLET:
[speaking to herself in the empty room, sounding deeply bored]
Well. At least I’m getting noticed. If only poor Frieda saw the light…
[END OF LOG]
`, } ]; // main ending picker function endingPicker = (out, totv, aa, quickstats) => { console.log("Preparing ending data..."); // for the School results window.cliqueData = calculateCliqueResults(); window.charlieWin = window.cliqueData.charlieWin; window.linusWin = window.cliqueData.linusWin; window.itsATie = window.cliqueData.itsATie; window.charlieCleanSweep = window.cliqueData.charlieCleanSweep; // queue the injection to run as soon as the DOM renders window.bobertTasks.push(() => { // clear out task once it has successfully rendered the table if (document.getElementById("custom-clique-table")) return true; const overallStats = document.getElementById("overall_vote_statistics"); // check if the container exists and we haven't already injected the table if (overallStats) { let wrapper = document.createElement("div"); wrapper.id = "custom-clique-table"; wrapper.innerHTML = window.cliqueData ? window.cliqueData.tableHTML : ""; // insert it right before the national results overallStats.parentNode.insertBefore(wrapper, overallStats); return true; } return false; }); // reset contingent mode when starting a new ending picker contingentMode = false; campaignTrail_temp.pages = []; campaignTrail_temp.header = ""; campaignTrail_temp.executable = {}; campaignTrail_temp.page = 0; campaignTrail_temp.image = false; const playerEV = quickstats[0]; const playerCandidateId = campaignTrail_temp.candidate_id; const answers = campaignTrail_temp.player_answers; // sort by electoral votes to ensure we handle any number of candidates correctly const sortedResults = [...aa].sort((a, b) => b.electoral_votes - a.electoral_votes); // find the player's position in the sorted results const playerIndex = sortedResults.findIndex(result => result.candidate === playerCandidateId); // get opponent EVs (that is, the candidate with most EVs who isn't the player) let opponentEVs = 0; let otherOpponentEVs = 0; if (playerIndex === 0) { // player is winning, get second and third place opponentEVs = sortedResults.length > 1 ? sortedResults[1].electoral_votes : 0; otherOpponentEVs = sortedResults.length > 2 ? sortedResults[2].electoral_votes : 0; } else { // player isn't winning, get first place and third place (or second non-player) opponentEVs = sortedResults[0].electoral_votes; // find the next highest candidate who isn't the player for (let i = 1; i < sortedResults.length; i++) { if (sortedResults[i].candidate !== playerCandidateId) { otherOpponentEVs = sortedResults[i].electoral_votes; break; } } } const IDS = { left: 79, right: 80, right2: 81 }; const resultsById = Object.fromEntries(aa.map(r => [r.candidate, r])); const candidatesById = Object.fromEntries( campaignTrail_temp.candidate_json.map(c => [c.pk, c]) ); // helper to get EV const getEV = (id) => resultsById[id]?.electoral_votes ?? 0; // helper to get full name const getName = (id) => { const c = candidatesById[id]; return c ? `${c.fields.first_name} ${c.fields.last_name}`.trim() : ""; }; // electoral votes const leftThirdEV = getEV(IDS.left); const rightThirdEV = getEV(IDS.right); const right2ThirdEV = getEV(IDS.right2); // names const leftNomineeName = getName(IDS.left); const rightNomineeName = getName(IDS.right); const right2NomineeName = getName(IDS.right2); // other achievement unlocks const ansSet = new Set(campaignTrail_temp.player_answers); const isCharlieWin = typeof charlieWin !== "undefined" && charlieWin; const isLinusWin = typeof linusWin !== "undefined" && linusWin; const achievements = [ { name: "Young Offender", cond: () => isCharlieWin }, { name: "Good Grief", cond: () => isLinusWin }, { name: "Next Sensation", cond: () => window.charlieCleanSweep }, { name: "Just Like You Imagined", cond: () => isCharlieWin && patty_approval < 0 }, { name: "Uncle Wiggily in Connecticut", cond: () => isCharlieWin && ruthlessness >= 5 }, { name: "A Different Corner", cond: () => isCharlieWin && ruthlessness <= 0 && linus_bond >= 3 }, { name: "The End Is Never...", cond: () => isLinusWin && ansSet.has(3314) && ansSet.has(3385) }, { name: "Love Like Blood", cond: () => isCharlieWin && window.isRoyInCharge && cynicism >= 5 }, { name: "60 Miles an Hour", cond: () => isCharlieWin && window.isRoyInCharge }, { name: "True Faith", cond: () => isCharlieWin && ruthlessness <= -2 }, { name: "Bohemian Like You", cond: () => isCharlieWin && support_arts >= 3 && (ansSet.has(3410) || ansSet.has(3411)) }, { name: "Primitive Notion", cond: () => isCharlieWin && support_jocks >= 3 && ansSet.has(3318) }, { name: "Running in the Family", cond: () => isCharlieWin && support_burnouts >= 3 && ansSet.has(3319) }, { name: "Kundalini Express", cond: () => isCharlieWin && ruthlessness >= 5 && cynicism >= 5 }, { name: "Erase and Rewind", cond: () => ansSet.has(3488) && cynicism <= 0 }, { name: "The Swing of Things", cond: () => anti_establishment >= 5 && support_admin <= -2 }, { name: "Bass to Mouth", cond: () => support_admin >= 2 && anti_establishment <= -1 }, ]; achievements.forEach(({ name, cond }) => { if (cond()) unlockAchievement(name); }); // completionist achievement const allKeys = Object.keys(campaignTrail_temp.achievements); // check if every achievement in the game (except this one, duh) // exists in the player's unlockedAchievements object let unlockedAchievements = JSON.parse(localStorage.getItem("unlockedAch")) || {}; const hasAll = allKeys.every(key => key === "Something Beautiful Remains" || unlockedAchievements[key]); if (hasAll) { unlockAchievement("Something Beautiful Remains"); } // recount checker window.isRecountElection = function (marginThreshold = 0.005) { const overall = [...e.final_overall_results].sort( (a, b) => b.electoral_votes - a.electoral_votes ); if (overall.length < 2) return false; const overallWinner = overall[0]; const overallRunnerUp = overall[1]; const evDiff = overallWinner.electoral_votes - overallRunnerUp.electoral_votes; for (const stateRes of e.final_state_results) { if (stateRes.result.length < 2) continue; const stateWinner = stateRes.result[0]; const stateRunnerUp = stateRes.result[1]; const margin = stateWinner.percent - stateRunnerUp.percent; const stateEV = stateWinner.electoral_votes || 0; // check if the margin is razor-thin if (margin <= marginThreshold) { // check if flipping this state alters the election outcome if ( stateWinner.candidate === overallWinner.candidate && stateRunnerUp.candidate === overallRunnerUp.candidate ) { if (evDiff <= 2 * stateEV) { return true; } } } } return false; }; // select main ending (slide 0) const selectedScenario = mainEndings.find(ending => ending.condition(playerEV, opponentEVs, rightThirdEV, leftThirdEV, leftNomineeName)); if (!selectedScenario) { console.error("No ending scenario matched."); return ""; } // track the ending song we should use let finalSong = selectedScenario.song || null; // get the raw content let pageContent = selectedScenario.html; // if it's a function (like in contingent_tie), execute it with the calculated stats if (typeof pageContent === 'function') { pageContent = pageContent(playerEV, opponentEVs, window.opLast || "Opponent"); } // push the result string campaignTrail_temp.pages.push(pageContent); // push main image campaignTrail_temp.executable[0] = () => { campaignTrail_temp.image = selectedScenario.image; if (typeof setImage === 'function') setImage(campaignTrail_temp.image); }; // select bonus slides (1+) // filter matching bonus slides const activeBonusSlides = bonusSlides.filter(slide => slide.condition(playerEV, opponentEVs, leftThirdEV, rightThirdEV, right2ThirdEV)); // calculate the starting index for bonus slides (0=Main, so 1 is next) let slideIndex = 1; activeBonusSlides.forEach(slide => { campaignTrail_temp.pages.push(slide.html); // overwrite if bonus slide has song if (slide.song) { finalSong = slide.song; } // we need to capture the current slideIndex in the closure const currentIdx = slideIndex; campaignTrail_temp.executable[currentIdx] = () => { campaignTrail_temp.image = slide.image; // ensure setImage is called if the user navigates here if (typeof setImage === 'function') setImage(campaignTrail_temp.image); }; slideIndex++; }); // play the selected ending song *once* if (finalSong && typeof window.playEndingSong === 'function') { const urlHash = JSON.stringify(finalSong.url); if (window.currentEndingSongUrl !== urlHash) { window.playEndingSong( finalSong.title, finalSong.artist, finalSong.cover, finalSong.url ); window.currentEndingSongUrl = urlHash; } } // render. finalizeRendering(); return ""; }; // helper to handle the observer logic function finalizeRendering() { const narrativeDiv = document.getElementById("endingtxt"); if (narrativeDiv) { narrativeDiv.innerHTML = ""; } if (attemptRenderEnding()) { return; } // do it if our container is missing window.bobertTasks.push(() => { if (document.getElementById("main-ending-scroll-container")) return true; return attemptRenderEnding(); }); if (window.bobert) { const targetNode = document.getElementById("game_window"); if (targetNode) { window.bobert.observe(targetNode, { childList: true, subtree: true }); } } } // The county map feature function getCustomPreference(key, defaultVal) { try { const prefs = JSON.parse(localStorage.getItem('tml_custom_preferences')) || {}; return prefs.hasOwnProperty(key) ? prefs[key] : defaultVal; } catch(e) { return defaultVal; } } // inject button into DOM function injectCountyMapButton() { if (!document.getElementById("county_map_button")) { const btn = document.createElement("button"); btn.className = "final_menu_button"; btn.id = "county_map_button"; btn.textContent = "County Map"; btn.onclick = countyMapScreenHtml; const ref = document.getElementById("final_election_map_button"); if (ref) ref.parentNode.insertBefore(btn, ref.nextSibling); } } // shared HTML chunks const MAP_CONTROLS_HTML = `

Loading map data (this may take a moment)...

`; function getMapInstructionsHtml(mode) { let baseZoomText = mode === 'seamless' ? "Scroll to zoom, click and drag to pan. Scroll all the way out to return to State Map." : "Scroll to zoom, click and drag to pan."; return `
Right-Click & Drag to paint. Click to paint county, Shift+Click for state. Drag map to pan.
${baseZoomText}
`; } const REDRAW_PANEL_HTML = `

🖍️ Redraw the States 0 / 538 EV

`; // inject into election map screen function injectCountyMapInPlace(mode) { const mapContainer = document.getElementById("map_container"); if (!mapContainer || document.getElementById("county_map_wrapper")) return; let toggleDiv, btnState, btnCounty; if (mode === 'together') { toggleDiv = document.createElement("div"); toggleDiv.style.cssText = "display: flex; justify-content: center; gap: 10px; margin-bottom: 10px;"; toggleDiv.innerHTML = ` `; mapContainer.parentNode.insertBefore(toggleDiv, mapContainer); btnState = toggleDiv.querySelector("#btn_show_state_map"); btnCounty = toggleDiv.querySelector("#btn_show_county_map"); } const originalChildren = Array.from(mapContainer.children); const countySvgContainer = document.createElement("div"); countySvgContainer.id = "county_map_wrapper"; countySvgContainer.style.display = "none"; countySvgContainer.style.width = "100%"; countySvgContainer.style.height = "100%"; countySvgContainer.style.position = "relative"; countySvgContainer.style.background = "#e2e6ea"; countySvgContainer.style.overflow = "hidden"; countySvgContainer.innerHTML = ` ${REDRAW_PANEL_HTML} `; mapContainer.appendChild(countySvgContainer); const controlsDiv = document.createElement("div"); controlsDiv.id = "county_map_bottom_controls"; controlsDiv.style.display = "none"; controlsDiv.style.marginTop = "10px"; controlsDiv.innerHTML = MAP_CONTROLS_HTML + getMapInstructionsHtml(mode); const mapFooter = document.getElementById("map_footer"); if (mapFooter) { mapFooter.parentNode.insertBefore(controlsDiv, mapFooter); } else { mapContainer.parentNode.insertBefore(controlsDiv, mapContainer.nextSibling); } let countyLoaded = false; window._switchToStateMap = function() { originalChildren.forEach(c => c.style.display = ''); countySvgContainer.style.display = "none"; controlsDiv.style.display = "none"; if (mode === 'together') { btnState.style.background = "#ddd"; btnState.style.color = "#000"; btnCounty.style.background = ""; btnCounty.style.color = ""; } }; window._switchToCountyMap = function(zoomX, zoomY) { originalChildren.forEach(c => c.style.display = 'none'); countySvgContainer.style.display = "block"; controlsDiv.style.display = "block"; if (mode === 'together') { btnCounty.style.background = "#ddd"; btnCounty.style.color = "#000"; btnState.style.background = ""; btnState.style.color = ""; } const handleZoom = () => { if (mode === 'seamless' && zoomX !== undefined && zoomY !== undefined && window._d3zoom) { const k = 2.5; const tx = zoomX - zoomX * k; const ty = zoomY - zoomY * k; d3.select("#county_svg").transition().duration(350).call( window._d3zoom.transform, d3.zoomIdentity.translate(tx, ty).scale(k) ); } }; if (!countyLoaded) { countyLoaded = true; loadAndDrawCountyMap(mode).then(handleZoom); } else { handleZoom(); } }; if (mode === 'together') { btnState.onclick = window._switchToStateMap; btnCounty.onclick = () => window._switchToCountyMap(); } else if (mode === 'seamless') { // desktop: mouse wheel zoom mapContainer.addEventListener("wheel", (e) => { if (countySvgContainer.style.display === "block") return; if (e.deltaY < 0) { e.preventDefault(); const rect = mapContainer.getBoundingClientRect(); const rx = ((e.clientX - rect.left) / rect.width) * 975; const ry = ((e.clientY - rect.top) / rect.height) * 610; window._switchToCountyMap(rx, ry); } }, {passive: false, capture: true}); // mobile: pinch-to-zoom and double-tap let initialPinchDist = null; let lastTap = 0; mapContainer.addEventListener("touchstart", (e) => { if (countySvgContainer.style.display === "block") return; // double tap to zoom const currentTime = new Date().getTime(); const tapLength = currentTime - lastTap; if (tapLength < 300 && tapLength > 0 && e.touches.length === 1) { e.preventDefault(); const rect = mapContainer.getBoundingClientRect(); const rx = ((e.touches[0].clientX - rect.left) / rect.width) * 975; const ry = ((e.touches[0].clientY - rect.top) / rect.height) * 610; window._switchToCountyMap(rx, ry); } lastTap = currentTime; // pinch to zoom initialize if (e.touches.length === 2) { initialPinchDist = Math.hypot( e.touches[0].clientX - e.touches[1].clientX, e.touches[0].clientY - e.touches[1].clientY ); } }, {passive: false}); mapContainer.addEventListener("touchmove", (e) => { if (countySvgContainer.style.display === "block" || !initialPinchDist) return; if (e.touches.length === 2) { const currentDist = Math.hypot( e.touches[0].clientX - e.touches[1].clientX, e.touches[0].clientY - e.touches[1].clientY ); // if user spreads fingers by more than 30px, zoom in if (currentDist > initialPinchDist + 30) { e.preventDefault(); initialPinchDist = null; const rect = mapContainer.getBoundingClientRect(); const centerX = (e.touches[0].clientX + e.touches[1].clientX) / 2; const centerY = (e.touches[0].clientY + e.touches[1].clientY) / 2; const rx = ((centerX - rect.left) / rect.width) * 975; const ry = ((centerY - rect.top) / rect.height) * 610; window._switchToCountyMap(rx, ry); } } }, {passive: false}); mapContainer.addEventListener("touchend", () => { initialPinchDist = null; }); } } // monkey-patch the core endgame screen functions const screensToWrap = [ "overallResultsHtml", "finalMapScreenHtml", "stateResultsHtml", "overallDetailsHtml", "furtherReadingHtml" ]; screensToWrap.forEach(fnName => { if (typeof window[fnName] === "function" && !window[fnName].patchedForCountyMap) { const originalFn = window[fnName]; window[fnName] = function(...args) { originalFn.apply(this, args); const mode = getCustomPreference('countyZoom', 'separate'); if (mode === 'separate') { setTimeout(injectCountyMapButton, 10); } else if (mode === 'together' || mode === 'seamless') { // only attach if we are exactly on the map screen if (fnName === 'finalMapScreenHtml') { setTimeout(() => injectCountyMapInPlace(mode), 10); } } }; window[fnName].patchedForCountyMap = true; } }); // setup separate screen mode function countyMapScreenHtml() { const gameHeader = document.querySelector(".game_header"); const headerHtml = gameHeader ? gameHeader.outerHTML : `
${window.corrr}
`; document.getElementById("game_window").innerHTML = ` ${headerHtml}

SIMULATED COUNTY RESULTS

${MAP_CONTROLS_HTML}
${REDRAW_PANEL_HTML}
${getMapInstructionsHtml('separate')}
`; document.getElementById("overall_results_button").onclick = window.overallResultsHtml; document.getElementById("final_election_map_button").onclick = window.finalMapScreenHtml; document.getElementById("state_results_button").onclick = window.stateResultsHtml; document.getElementById("overall_details_button").onclick = window.overallDetailsHtml; document.getElementById("play_again_button").onclick = window.beginNewGameHtml; if(document.getElementById("recommended_reading_button") && window.furtherReadingHtml) { document.getElementById("recommended_reading_button").onclick = window.furtherReadingHtml; } loadAndDrawCountyMap('separate'); } // the actual county map rendering function const _countyMapCache = { data: null, data1996: null, us: null }; async function loadAndDrawCountyMap(mode) { const scriptsToLoad = []; if (!window.d3) scriptsToLoad.push($.getScript("https://d3js.org/d3.v7.min.js")); if (!window.topojson) scriptsToLoad.push($.getScript("https://d3js.org/topojson.v3.min.js")); if (scriptsToLoad.length > 0) await Promise.all(scriptsToLoad); try { let countyResultsRaw, countyResults1996Raw, us; const electionData = window.campaignTrail_temp || window.e; // perform fetch and heavy pre-processing only if cache is empty if (!_countyMapCache.data || !_countyMapCache.data1996 || !_countyMapCache.us) { const countyDataUrl = "https://raw.githubusercontent.com/StrawberryMaster/StrawberryMaster.github.io/refs/heads/master/scripts/county_results_2000.min.json"; const countyData1996Url = "https://raw.githubusercontent.com/StrawberryMaster/StrawberryMaster.github.io/refs/heads/master/scripts/county_results_1996.min.json"; const usMapUrl = "https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/us-atlas@3/counties-albers-10m.json"; const [raw, raw1996, usData] = await Promise.all([ fetch(countyDataUrl).then(r => r.json()), fetch(countyData1996Url).then(r => r.json()).catch(() => []), fetch(usMapUrl).then(r => r.json()) ]); // Shannon County, SD --> Oglala Lakota const shannon = raw.find(c => c.fips === "46113"); if (shannon) shannon.fips = "46102"; const shannon96 = raw1996.find(c => c.fips === "46113"); if (shannon96) shannon96.fips = "46102"; // synthesize modern Alaska boroughs // basically a rough guess to get them working in the map const akBoroughs = { "02020": { name: "Anchorage", d:["02010","02011","02012","02014","02015","02016","02017","02018","02019","02021","02022","02023","02024","02025"] }, "02170": { name: "Matanuska-Susitna", d:["02026","02027","02028"] }, "02090": { name: "Fairbanks North Star", d:["02029","02030","02031","02032","02033"] }, "02110": { name: "Juneau", d:["02003","02004"] }, "02122": { name: "Kenai Peninsula", d:["02007","02008","02009"] }, "02150": { name: "Kodiak Island", d: ["02006"] }, "02130": { name: "Ketchikan Gateway", d:["02001"] }, "02198": { name: "Prince of Wales-Hyder", d:["02001"] }, "02275": { name: "Wrangell", d:["02001"] }, "02220": { name: "Sitka", d: ["02002"] }, "02195": { name: "Petersburg", d: ["02002"] }, "02282": { name: "Yakutat", d: ["02005"] }, "02100": { name: "Haines", d: ["02005"] }, "02230": { name: "Skagway", d: ["02005"] }, "02105": { name: "Hoonah-Angoon", d:["02005"] }, "02063": { name: "Chugach", d:["02034"] }, "02066": { name: "Copper River", d:["02034"] }, "02261": { name: "Valdez-Cordova", d:["02034"] }, "02068": { name: "Denali", d:["02034"] }, "02240": { name: "Southeast Fairbanks", d:["02035"] }, "02290": { name: "Yukon-Koyukuk", d: ["02036"] }, "02188": { name: "Northwest Arctic", d: ["02037"] }, "02185": { name: "North Slope", d: ["02037"] }, "02180": { name: "Nome", d: ["02038"] }, "02050": { name: "Bethel", d:["02039"] }, "02158": { name: "Kusilvak", d:["02039"] }, "02013": { name: "Aleutians East", d:["02040"] }, "02016": { name: "Aleutians West", d: ["02040"] }, "02060": { name: "Bristol Bay", d: ["02040"] }, "02070": { name: "Dillingham", d: ["02040"] }, "02164": { name: "Lake and Peninsula", d: ["02040"] } }; // process Alaska const akDistricts = {}; raw.forEach(c => { if (c.state_po === "AK" && c.year === "2000") akDistricts[String(c.fips).padStart(5, '0')] = c; }); const newAkBoroughs = []; Object.keys(akBoroughs).forEach(bFips => { const bInfo = akBoroughs[bFips]; let tDem = 0, tRep = 0, tOth = 0, tAll = 0; bInfo.d.forEach(distFips => { const dData = akDistricts[distFips]; if (dData) { tDem += dData.votes_dem || 0; tRep += dData.votes_rep || 0; tOth += dData.votes_other || 0; tAll += dData.votes_all || 0; } }); if (tAll > 0) { newAkBoroughs.push({ fips: bFips, county_name: bInfo.name, state_po: "AK", year: "2000", votes_dem: tDem, votes_rep: tRep, votes_other: tOth, votes_all: tAll }); } }); let filtered2000 = raw.filter(c => !(c.state_po === "AK" && String(c.fips).startsWith("02") && c.county_name.includes("DISTRICT"))); filtered2000.push(...newAkBoroughs); // AK 1996 processing const akDistricts96 = {}; raw1996.forEach(c => { if (c.state_po === "AK" && c.year === "1996") akDistricts96[String(c.fips).padStart(5, '0')] = c; }); const newAkBoroughs96 = []; Object.keys(akBoroughs).forEach(bFips => { const bInfo = akBoroughs[bFips]; let tDem = 0, tRep = 0, tOth = 0, tAll = 0; bInfo.d.forEach(distFips => { const dData = akDistricts96[distFips]; if (dData) { tDem += dData.votes_dem || 0; tRep += dData.votes_rep || 0; tOth += dData.votes_other || 0; tAll += dData.votes_all || 0; } }); if (tAll > 0) { newAkBoroughs96.push({ fips: bFips, county_name: bInfo.name, state_po: "AK", year: "1996", votes_dem: tDem, votes_rep: tRep, votes_other: tOth, votes_all: tAll }); } }); let filtered1996 = raw1996.filter(c => !(c.state_po === "AK" && String(c.fips).startsWith("02") && c.county_name.includes("DISTRICT"))); filtered1996.push(...newAkBoroughs96); // pre-standardize and pad FIPS keys filtered2000.forEach(c => c.fips = String(c.fips).padStart(5, '0')); filtered1996.forEach(c => c.fips = String(c.fips).padStart(5, '0')); // cache the final processed arrays _countyMapCache.data = filtered2000; _countyMapCache.data1996 = filtered1996; _countyMapCache.us = usData; } // assign raw references directly countyResultsRaw = _countyMapCache.data; countyResults1996Raw = _countyMapCache.data1996; us = _countyMapCache.us; // pre-process 1996 dataset const base1996Map = {}; for (let i = 0, len = countyResults1996Raw.length; i < len; i++) { const c = countyResults1996Raw[i]; if (c.year === "1996") { base1996Map[c.fips] = c; } } // pre-process 2000 dataset const base2000Map = {}; const stateBase = {}; let nationalTotalVotes = 0; for (let i = 0, len = countyResultsRaw.length; i < len; i++) { const c = countyResultsRaw[i]; if (c.year === "2000") { base2000Map[c.fips] = c; const st = c.state_po; if (!stateBase[st]) stateBase[st] = { dem: 0, rep: 0, other: 0, all: 0 }; stateBase[st].dem += (c.votes_dem || 0); stateBase[st].rep += (c.votes_rep || 0); stateBase[st].other += (c.votes_other || 0); stateBase[st].all += (c.votes_all || 0); nationalTotalVotes += (c.votes_all || 0); } } const candsInfo = PROPS.CANDIDATES; const activeCandidates = electionData.final_overall_results.map(r => r.candidate); const topCands = [...electionData.final_overall_results].sort((a,b) => b.popular_votes - a.popular_votes); if (topCands.length < 2) throw new Error("Could not find two main candidates."); let demCandId = topCands[0].candidate, repCandId = topCands[1].candidate; const cand0Data = electionData.candidate_json.find(c => c.pk === demCandId)?.fields || {}; const cand1Data = electionData.candidate_json.find(c => c.pk === repCandId)?.fields || {}; const party0 = (cand0Data.party || "").toLowerCase(); const party1 = (cand1Data.party || "").toLowerCase(); const is0Dem = party0.includes("democrat"); const is1Dem = party1.includes("democrat"); const is0Rep = party0.includes("republican"); const is1Rep = party1.includes("republican"); if ((is0Dem && is1Rep) || (is1Dem && is0Rep)) { if (is1Dem) { demCandId = topCands[1].candidate; repCandId = topCands[0].candidate; } } else { const stateNY = electionData.final_state_results.find(s => s.abbr === "NY"); const stateTX = electionData.final_state_results.find(s => s.abbr === "TX"); if (stateNY && stateTX && (stateTX.result.find(r => r.candidate === demCandId)?.percent || 0) > (stateNY.result.find(r => r.candidate === demCandId)?.percent || 0)) { repCandId = topCands[0].candidate; demCandId = topCands[1].candidate; } } const demName = candsInfo.get(String(demCandId))?.last_name || "Democrat"; const repName = candsInfo.get(String(repCandId))?.last_name || "Republican"; const demColor = candsInfo.get(String(demCandId))?.color_hex || "#0000ff"; const repColor = candsInfo.get(String(repCandId))?.color_hex || "#ff0000"; const stateSwings = {}; electionData.final_state_results.forEach(f => { const base = stateBase[f.abbr]; const swings = {}; f.result.forEach(r => { let basePct = 0; if (base && base.all > 0) { if (r.candidate === demCandId) basePct = base.dem / base.all; else if (r.candidate === repCandId) basePct = base.rep / base.all; else basePct = (base.other / base.all) / Math.max(1, activeCandidates.length - 2); } swings[r.candidate] = (r.percent || 0) - basePct; }); stateSwings[f.abbr] = swings; }); const colorInterpolators = {}; activeCandidates.forEach(candId => { colorInterpolators[candId] = d3.interpolateRgb("#ffffff", candsInfo.get(String(candId))?.color_hex || "#888888"); }); const usCounties = topojson.feature(us, us.objects.counties).features; const geomFips = us.objects.counties.geometries.map(g => String(g.id).padStart(5, '0')); const fipsToGeomIdx = {}; geomFips.forEach((f, i) => fipsToGeomIdx[f] = i); const neighborData = topojson.neighbors(us.objects.counties.geometries); const path = d3.geoPath(); let maxMarginVotes = 0; const densityScale = d3.scaleLog().domain([1000, 200000]).range([0, 1]).clamp(true); // caching pass const unnormalizedCounties = {}; const simStateVotes = {}; // target percentages straight from the game results const targetStatePct = {}; electionData.final_state_results.forEach(f => { targetStatePct[f.abbr] = {}; f.result.forEach(r => { targetStatePct[f.abbr][r.candidate] = r.percent || 0; }); }); // calculate raw swung baseline and accumulate simulated state totals usCounties.forEach(d => { d.fips = String(d.id).padStart(5, '0'); const c = base2000Map[d.fips]; if (!c) return; const total = c.votes_all || 1; const swings = stateSwings[c.state_po] || {}; const countyResults = activeCandidates.map(candId => { let baseVotes = (candId === demCandId) ? (c.votes_dem || 0) : (candId === repCandId ? (c.votes_rep || 0) : ((c.votes_other || 0) / Math.max(1, activeCandidates.length - 2))); let basePct = baseVotes / total; let rawSwing = swings[candId] || 0; // swing math (dampened to avoid going below 0 or above 100) let dampenedSwing = rawSwing > 0 ? rawSwing * (1 - basePct) * 1.2 : rawSwing * basePct * 1.2; let dynamicCap = Math.max(0.15, Math.abs(rawSwing) * 1.75); dampenedSwing = Math.max(-dynamicCap, Math.min(dynamicCap, dampenedSwing)); let newPct = Math.max(0, Math.min(1, basePct + dampenedSwing)); return { id: candId, pct: newPct, votes: newPct * total, baseVotes: baseVotes }; }); // make sure the raw estimates sum to 100% locally const sumPct = countyResults.reduce((sum, cr) => sum + cr.pct, 0); if (sumPct > 0) { countyResults.forEach(cr => { cr.pct = cr.pct / sumPct; cr.votes = cr.pct * total; }); } unnormalizedCounties[d.fips] = { results: countyResults, total: total, state_po: c.state_po }; // tally up simulated state totals if (!simStateVotes[c.state_po]) simStateVotes[c.state_po] = { total: 0 }; simStateVotes[c.state_po].total += total; countyResults.forEach(cr => { simStateVotes[c.state_po][cr.id] = (simStateVotes[c.state_po][cr.id] || 0) + cr.votes; }); }); // apply state-level correction multipliers and finalize usCounties.forEach(d => { const c = base2000Map[d.fips]; const unnorm = unnormalizedCounties[d.fips]; if (!c || !unnorm) return; const total = unnorm.total; const st = c.state_po; const targetPcts = targetStatePct[st] || {}; const simVotes = simStateVotes[st] || {}; const stateTotalVotes = simVotes.total || 1; // normalize each candidate's votes against the game's actual state percentage const normalizedResults = unnorm.results.map(cr => { const targetPct = targetPcts[cr.id] || 0; const simPct = (simVotes[cr.id] || 0) / stateTotalVotes; let normVotes = cr.votes; if (simPct > 0 && targetPct > 0) { normVotes = cr.votes * (targetPct / simPct); } else if (simPct === 0 && targetPct > 0) { // edge case: county swung to 0, but state has votes, so re-distribute proportionately. normVotes = unnorm.total * targetPct; } else if (targetPct === 0) { normVotes = 0; } return { ...cr, normVotes: normVotes }; }); // rescale finalized votes to exactly equal the county's total turnout capacity const normTotal = normalizedResults.reduce((sum, cr) => sum + cr.normVotes, 0); let finalResults = []; if (normTotal > 0) { finalResults = normalizedResults.map(cr => { const finalPct = cr.normVotes / normTotal; return { id: cr.id, pct: finalPct, votes: Math.round(finalPct * unnorm.total), baseVotes: cr.baseVotes }; }); } else { finalResults = unnorm.results.map(cr => ({ ...cr, votes: Math.round(cr.votes) })); } // determine the IRL vs simulated winners let irlWinner = finalResults.reduce((prev, current) => (prev.baseVotes > current.baseVotes) ? prev : current); finalResults.sort((a, b) => b.pct - a.pct); const winner = finalResults[0]; const flipped = irlWinner.id !== winner.id; const marginPct = winner.pct - (finalResults.length > 1 ? finalResults[1].pct : 0); const marginVotesEst = winner.votes - (finalResults.length > 1 ? finalResults[1].votes : 0); if (marginVotesEst > maxMarginVotes) maxMarginVotes = marginVotesEst; const candColor = candsInfo.get(String(winner.id))?.color_hex || "#888"; // build bivariate density palette const dens = densityScale(total); // ensure even razor-thin margins retain a distinct tint of the candidate's color const colorWeight = 0.35 + 0.65 * Math.min(1, marginPct * 3.0); // high density: dark gray + candidate color const maxDensityColor = d3.interpolateRgb("#555555", candColor)(colorWeight); // low density: white + candidate color const minDensityColor = d3.interpolateRgb("#ffffff", candColor)(colorWeight * 0.4); const densityColor = d3.interpolateRgb(minDensityColor, maxDensityColor)(dens); // shift calculations const c1996 = base1996Map[d.fips]; let shiftHtml = ""; let colorShift = "#ccc"; if (c1996 && c1996.votes_all > 0) { let histDemPct = (c1996.votes_dem || 0) / c1996.votes_all; let histRepPct = (c1996.votes_rep || 0) / c1996.votes_all; let histMargin = histDemPct - histRepPct; let simDemPct = 0, simRepPct = 0; finalResults.forEach(r => { if (r.id === demCandId) simDemPct = r.pct; if (r.id === repCandId) simRepPct = r.pct; }); let simMargin = simDemPct - simRepPct; let shift = simMargin - histMargin; // positive = Dem shift, negative = Rep shift let shiftIntensity = Math.min(Math.abs(shift) * 3, 1); // max out visual at 33.3% shift if (shift > 0) { colorShift = d3.interpolateRgb("#ffffff", demColor)(shiftIntensity); shiftHtml = `
Shift: ${demName} +${(shift*100).toFixed(1)}%
`; } else { colorShift = d3.interpolateRgb("#ffffff", repColor)(shiftIntensity); shiftHtml = `
Shift: ${repName} +${(Math.abs(shift)*100).toFixed(1)}%
`; } } else { shiftHtml = `
No 1996 data available
`; } d.proj = { name: c.county_name, state: c.state_po, results: finalResults, totalVotes: unnorm.total, marginVotes: marginVotesEst, winnerId: winner.id, colorChoro: colorInterpolators[winner.id](Math.sqrt(marginPct)), colorShare: colorInterpolators[winner.id](winner.pct), colorBinary: candColor, colorDensity: densityColor, colorFlipped: flipped ? candColor : "#ececec", colorShift: colorShift, winText: `${candsInfo.get(String(winner.id))?.last_name} +${(marginPct * 100).toFixed(1)}% (${marginVotesEst.toLocaleString()} votes)` }; d.proj.tooltipHtml = `${d.proj.name}, ${d.proj.state}
Total Votes: ${unnorm.total.toLocaleString()}
` + finalResults.slice(0,3).map(r => `
${candsInfo.get(String(r.id))?.last_name}: ${(r.pct * 100).toFixed(1)}% (${r.votes.toLocaleString()})
`).join('') + `
Margin: ${d.proj.winText}
` + (flipped ? `
Flipped from ${candsInfo.get(String(irlWinner.id))?.last_name}
` : `
Held by ${candsInfo.get(String(winner.id))?.last_name}
`) + shiftHtml; const cent = path.centroid(d); d.centroid = (isFinite(cent[0]) && isFinite(cent[1]) && (cent[0] !== 0 || cent[1] !== 0)) ? cent : null; }); const radiusScale = d3.scaleSqrt().domain([0, maxMarginVotes]).range([0, 35]); usCounties.forEach(d => { if (d.proj && d.centroid) d.baseR = radiusScale(d.proj.marginVotes); }); // map setup const svg = d3.select("#county_svg"); const g = svg.append("g").attr("id", "county_map_g"); let currentZoomK = 1; let currentMode = "choropleth"; svg.on("contextmenu", (e) => e.preventDefault()); let tooltip = d3.select("#county_tooltip"); if (tooltip.empty()) tooltip = d3.select("body").append("div").attr("id", "county_tooltip").attr("style", "position:absolute; background:rgba(0,0,0,0.9); color:#fff; padding:12px; border-radius:6px; pointer-events:none; display:none; font-size:14px; font-family:sans-serif; z-index:999999; white-space:nowrap; border:1px solid #666; box-shadow:0px 4px 8px rgba(0,0,0,0.5);"); const highlightPath = g.append("path").attr("id", "county-highlight").attr("fill", "none").attr("stroke", "#000").attr("stroke-width", "1.5").attr("vector-effect", "non-scaling-stroke").style("pointer-events", "none").style("display", "none"); // redraw the states special logic let customStates = {}; let countyToCustom = {}; let activeRS = null; let rsCounter = 1; let isPainting = false; let allocatedEVs = 0; let activePaintColor = "#aaa"; let rsStateCentroids = {}; function recalcRSStats() { Object.values(customStates).forEach(s => { s.tVotes = 0; s.cands = {}; s.ev = 0; }); const stateCentroids = {}; let totalPaintedVotes = 0; usCounties.forEach(c => { const sid = countyToCustom[c.fips]; if (!sid) return; // tally votes if the custom state and project data are valid if (customStates[sid] && c.proj) { customStates[sid].tVotes += c.proj.totalVotes; c.proj.results.forEach(r => { customStates[sid].cands[r.id] = (customStates[sid].cands[r.id] || 0) + r.votes; }); } // tally coordinate weights for dynamic label centroids if (c.centroid) { if (!stateCentroids[sid]) { stateCentroids[sid] = { x: 0, y: 0, w: 0 }; } const w = c.proj ? c.proj.totalVotes : 1; stateCentroids[sid].x += c.centroid[0] * w; stateCentroids[sid].y += c.centroid[1] * w; stateCentroids[sid].w += w; } }); rsStateCentroids = stateCentroids; Object.values(customStates).forEach(s => { totalPaintedVotes += s.tVotes; if (s.tVotes === 0) { s.color = "#888"; s.marginText = "Empty"; s.winName = ""; s.winnerId = null; } else { const arr = Object.keys(s.cands).map(id => ({ id, v: s.cands[id], pct: s.cands[id] / s.tVotes })).sort((a,b) => b.v - a.v); const margin = arr[0].pct - (arr[1] ? arr[1].pct : 0); s.color = colorInterpolators[arr[0].id](Math.min(margin * 1.5, 1)); s.winName = candsInfo.get(String(arr[0].id))?.last_name || "Win"; s.winnerId = arr[0].id; s.marginText = `+${(margin * 100).toFixed(1)}%`; } }); // EV apportionment allocatedEVs = 0; let totalAllocated = 0; const stateRemainders = []; const realStateEVs = {}; electionData.states_json.forEach(s => { realStateEVs["rs_" + s.fields.abbr] = s.fields.electoral_votes; }); Object.values(customStates).forEach(s => { if (s.tVotes === 0) { s.ev = 0; return; } if (realStateEVs[s.id] !== undefined) { s.ev = realStateEVs[s.id]; totalAllocated += s.ev; } else { const exactEV = (s.tVotes / nationalTotalVotes) * 538; s.ev = Math.floor(exactEV); totalAllocated += s.ev; stateRemainders.push({ id: s.id, remainder: exactEV - s.ev }); } }); stateRemainders.sort((a, b) => b.remainder - a.remainder); let targetTotalEVs = Math.min(538, Math.round((totalPaintedVotes / nationalTotalVotes) * 538)); let remainingToDistribute = targetTotalEVs - totalAllocated; for (let i = 0; i < remainingToDistribute && i < stateRemainders.length; i++) { customStates[stateRemainders[i].id].ev += 1; } Object.values(customStates).forEach(s => { allocatedEVs += s.ev; }); const evTracker = document.getElementById("rs_ev_tracker"); if (evTracker) evTracker.innerText = `${allocatedEVs} / 538 EV`; // labels const labelData = Object.values(customStates).map(s => { const cen = stateCentroids[s.id]; return { ...s, cx: cen ? cen.x / cen.w : null, cy: cen ? cen.y / cen.w : null }; }).filter(s => s.cx !== null); const lblSel = customLabelsG.selectAll("g.rs-label").data(labelData, d => d.id); lblSel.exit().remove(); const lblEnter = lblSel.enter().append("g").attr("class", "rs-label"); lblEnter.append("text").attr("class", "rs-lbl-name").attr("text-anchor", "middle").attr("dy", "-0.2em") .style("font-family", "Arial, sans-serif").style("font-weight", "bold").style("fill", "#000") .style("paint-order", "stroke").style("stroke", "#fff").style("stroke-width", "3px").style("stroke-linejoin", "round"); lblEnter.append("text").attr("class", "rs-lbl-ev").attr("text-anchor", "middle").attr("dy", "0.9em") .style("font-family", "Arial, sans-serif").style("fill", "#000") .style("paint-order", "stroke").style("stroke", "#fff").style("stroke-width", "2px").style("stroke-linejoin", "round"); const lblMerged = lblSel.merge(lblEnter); lblMerged.attr("transform", d => `translate(${d.cx},${d.cy})`); lblMerged.select(".rs-lbl-name").text(d => d.name).style("font-size", `${Math.max(4, 11 / Math.sqrt(currentZoomK))}px`); lblMerged.select(".rs-lbl-ev").text(d => d.ev > 0 ? `${d.ev} EV` : "").style("font-size", `${Math.max(3, 9 / Math.sqrt(currentZoomK))}px`); updateRSResults(); } function recalcRSBorders() { const mesh = topojson.mesh(us, us.objects.counties, (a, b) => { const cA = countyToCustom[String(a.id).padStart(5, '0')]; const cB = countyToCustom[String(b.id).padStart(5, '0')]; return cA !== cB; }); d3.select("#custom-borders-path").datum(mesh).attr("d", path); } function recalcAllRS() { recalcRSStats(); recalcRSBorders(); } function renderRSPanel() { const list = document.getElementById("redraw_list"); if (!list) return; const existing = new Map([...list.querySelectorAll("[data-rs-id]")].map(el => [el.dataset.rsId, el])); const sortMode = document.getElementById("rs_sort_mode") ? document.getElementById("rs_sort_mode").value : "id"; let statesArr = Object.values(customStates); if (sortMode === "name") { statesArr.sort((a,b) => a.name.localeCompare(b.name)); } else if (sortMode === "ev") { statesArr.sort((a,b) => b.ev - a.ev); } else { statesArr.sort((a,b) => { const numA = parseInt(a.id.replace('rs_', '')) || 0; const numB = parseInt(b.id.replace('rs_', '')) || 0; return numA - numB; }); } statesArr.forEach(s => { let div = existing.get(s.id); if (!div) { div = document.createElement("div"); div.dataset.rsId = s.id; div.style.cssText = `display:flex; align-items:center; padding:6px; margin-bottom:4px; border:1px solid #ddd; background:#fff; cursor:pointer; border-radius:4px; font-size:12px;`; div.innerHTML = `
${s.ev} EV  |  ${s.winName} ${s.marginText}
`; div.onclick = () => { activeRS = s.id; renderRSPanel(); }; div.querySelector(".rs-color-dot").onclick = (e) => { e.stopPropagation(); activeRS = s.id; renderRSPanel(); if (rsStateCentroids[s.id]) { const cx = rsStateCentroids[s.id].x / rsStateCentroids[s.id].w; const cy = rsStateCentroids[s.id].y / rsStateCentroids[s.id].w; const svgNode = document.getElementById("county_svg"); const width = svgNode.clientWidth || 975; const height = svgNode.clientHeight || 610; const k = Math.max(3, currentZoomK); // zoom in a bit automatically svg.transition().duration(750).call(zoom.transform, d3.zoomIdentity.translate(width/2 - cx*k, height/2 - cy*k).scale(k)); } }; const inputEl = div.querySelector(".rs-name-input"); inputEl.value = s.name; inputEl.onclick = (e) => e.stopPropagation(); inputEl.addEventListener("input", (e) => s.name = e.target.value); list.appendChild(div); } else { existing.delete(s.id); list.appendChild(div); } // always update dynamic parts div.style.border = `1px solid ${activeRS === s.id ? '#000' : '#ddd'}`; div.style.background = activeRS === s.id ? '#eef5ff' : '#fff'; div.querySelector(".rs-color-dot").style.background = s.color; div.querySelector(".rs-ev-label").textContent = `${s.ev} EV \u00a0|\u00a0 ${s.winName} ${s.marginText}`; div.querySelector(".rs-name-input").value = s.name; }); existing.forEach(el => el.remove()); } function updateRSResults() { const panel = document.getElementById("rs_results_panel"); if (!panel || Object.keys(customStates).length === 0) { if(panel) panel.style.display="none"; return; } // tally EVs per candidate const evTotals = {}; Object.values(customStates).forEach(s => { if (!s.winnerId || s.ev === 0) return; evTotals[s.winnerId] = (evTotals[s.winnerId] || 0) + s.ev; }); const sorted = Object.entries(evTotals).sort((a,b) => b[1] - a[1]); const winner270 = sorted.find(([,ev]) => ev >= 270); let html = `
Electoral Result
`; sorted.forEach(([candId, ev]) => { const info = candsInfo.get(String(candId)); const isWinner = winner270 && winner270[0] === candId; html += `
${info?.last_name||candId} ${ev} EV ${isWinner ? '✓ WINS' : ''}
`; }); const unallocated = 538 - Object.values(evTotals).reduce((a,b)=>a+b,0); if (unallocated > 0) html += `
Unallocated: ${unallocated} EV
`; panel.innerHTML = html; panel.style.display = "block"; } const deleteRS = (id) => { delete customStates[id]; if (activeRS === id) activeRS = Object.keys(customStates)[0] || null; Object.keys(countyToCustom).forEach(fips => { if (countyToCustom[fips] === id) delete countyToCustom[fips]; }); refreshMapFills(); renderRSPanel(); }; const redrawList = document.getElementById("redraw_list"); if(redrawList) { redrawList.addEventListener("click", e => { const delBtn = e.target.closest("[data-rs-del]"); if (delBtn) { e.stopPropagation(); deleteRS(delBtn.dataset.rsDel); } }); } const btnNew = document.getElementById("rs_btn_new"); if(btnNew) btnNew.onclick = () => { const id = "rs_" + rsCounter++; customStates[id] = { id, name: "State " + (rsCounter-1), tVotes:0, cands:{}, ev:0, color:"transparent", marginText:"", winName:"" }; activeRS = id; renderRSPanel(); }; const btnClear = document.getElementById("rs_btn_clear"); if(btnClear) btnClear.onclick = () => { customStates = {}; countyToCustom = {}; activeRS = null; refreshMapFills(); renderRSPanel(); }; const btnExisting = document.getElementById("rs_btn_existing"); if(btnExisting) btnExisting.onclick = () => { customStates = {}; countyToCustom = {}; usCounties.forEach(c => { if (!c.proj) return;
